content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Leslie Ann Carpentiers 
Deputy City Clerk 
450 San Rafael Avenue 
Belvedere, CA 
94920 
Dear Ms. Carpentiers: 
September 7, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 88-350 
" 
Your letter requesting advice concerning the recently 
enacted campaign finance reform initiatives (Government Code 
section 85100, et ~) was received on September 6, 1988 by 
the Fair Political Practices commission. 
If you have any 
questions about your advice request, you may contact John 
McLean, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
Because of the delayed operative date of most of the 
provisions of these initiatives, and the numerous inquiries we 
are receiving relative to implementation, we are attempting to 
respond to questions regarding interpretation in an organized 
and efficient fashion. 
Due to the volume of requests and 
complexity of the issues involved, we will answer your question 
in a timely manner, but not within the usual twenty-one working 
days. 
(Government Code Section 83114(b).) 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
You also should be aware 
that your letter and our response are public records which may 
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
GWB:plh:3 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) ~22~5660 


C:ITY of BELVEDERE 


State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
: File 
Date 
October 28, 1988 
From 
: 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
John McLean 
Subject: T-88-350 
Spoke today with Leslie Ann Carpentiers, Deputy city Clerk 
in Belvedere. 
Informed her about Emergency Regulation 18901 
and the permanent regulation which the Commission will hear in 
December. 
Specifically informed her that: 
1. 
Newsletters and mass mailings could not include 
articles written by Councilmembers if the articles listed the 
councilmembers as authors or contained the name, signature or 
other reference to an elected officer. 
2. 
Newsletter articles on council actions could not 
include a name, signature or other reference to an elected 
officer. 
3. 
Newsletters may not contain photos of elected officers. 
4. 
Newsletters may contain a roster listing of elected 
officers in which all names appear in the same type size and 
typeface. 


BELVEDERE 
NEWSLETTER 
City of Belvedere • Incorporated 1897 
450 SAN RAFAEL AVENUE 
BELVEDERE, CALIFORNIA 94920 
Goals, Priorities and 
ments 
Let's go in this direction 
Shortly after the Council election 
April, the new City Council met to 
discuss goals and priorities for the 
upcoming fiscal year and to deter-
mine Council assignments to various 
governmental committees and to 
various areas of City operations. 
Following several hours of dis-
cussion, the City 
decided 
that the following were of top prior-
ity: continued emphasis on improve-
ments to the City storm drain system; 
exploration of Belvedere's participa-
tion in 
efforts on the Tiburon penllnslula; 
velopment of a new pelummlll-
emergency 
improvements to 
traffic safety; and completion of 
review 
the zoning and design 
review ordinances. 
Other issues 
acquisition 
of open space 
to Old St. 
Hilary's and the need for more 
recreational opportunities for chil-
dren and adults. 
The following areas of responsi-

fiscal year wruch was prepared by the 
City Manager. City Manager San

Districts, as well as the General

and Open Space; Vice-Mayor Valen-

Belvedere Avenue. 
for the 


Storm Drain Survey 
In the next several months, you may 
see a funny looking van parked on your 
street with a technician sitting in the back 
at a control console. What that 
technician will be doing is video-taping 
the existing condition of a number of the 
City's storm drains. 
These storm drains collect and carry 
storm runoff. This is the fIrst step in a 
comprehensive approach to determine the 
condition of these storm drains. 
Considering the potential for property 
damage that may result whenever a storm 
drain fails, the City is quite anxious to get 
this survey done as quickly as possible so 
that necessary repairs can be undertaken. 
If you see the van, don't hesitate to 
stop and look, and you may see 
something unlike anything on the 
networks today. 
Master Plan Solves Staff Space Problems 
Knock, Knock, 
Who's There? 
On occasion you may fInd yourself 
answering your front door and being met 
by a person who says, "Hello, I'm from 
the Ozone Society, and I would like to 
raise your social consciousness by 
emptying your pocketbook." If someone 
comes to your door that you feel uncom-
fortable about, please call the Police De-
partment and make sure they have 
clearance to be in your neighborhood. 
The City cannot keep members of bona 
fIde charities from soliciting door-to-
door, but they do need to be registered 
with the Police Department 
Peddlers who go door-to-door selling 
items for a profIt do need a license but 
rarely apply for one. If someone comes 
to your door to sen you something, please 
can 435-2611 and let the Police Depart-
ment know so we can be sure they have a 
permit. 
City Administrative Offices to be Remodeled 
The existing administrative offices in 
City Han were designed without benefIt 
of a master plan, and staff has been using 
space which was originally intended for 
other purposes. As soon as the Commu-
nity Center renovation is completed 
downstairs, the implementation of a new 
master plan for existing City Hall office 
space will begin. The new plan will be 
Got A Spare 
Computer? 
Do you happen to have a computer sitting 
around the house just 
dust?

much more flexible in nature and can be 
adapted in the future to meet changing 
needs. 
The upstairs, which used to serve as 
the Police Department squad room, is 
being converted to increase storage 
capacity for City records and to provide a 
conference room for use by staff and 
various City committees. 
Find A Hole 
And Fill It 
Whenever the City has undertaken a 
street rehabilitation 
residents 
have asked if the 
would 
Once the remodeling is completed, 
you will be able to come in to City Hall 
and meet with a specific staff person 
without having to bother other staffers 
who may be working nearby. It will also 
provide a much more pleasant working 
environment for City staffers who spend 
long hours serving the needs of the 
community.

privately. It is understood that the City 
will not be involved in negotiating or 
paying for any work done on private 
property. Please call City Hall if you 
would like to be added to this list 


City of Belvedere 
Volunteer Service Award 
Gloria Lipschultz 
Belvedere's Favorite Hostess 
The warmth of a community is measured by its 
heart. When a small town makes a visitor feel 
welcome, or provides a child with happy memories 
shared with friends and family, you can bet that town 
has someone living there with 
the generous spirit of Belvedere 's 
Gloria Lipschultz. 
On August 1, 1988 Gloria 
received an honor reserved just 
for those Belvedere citizens who 
quietly and continuously give of 
their personal time for the benefit 
of the community, a special 
volunteer appreciation reward. 
This pretty lady with the 
laughing green eyes loves to see 
people enjoying community 
events. To this end, she has often 
volunteered to plan and/or provide refreshments for 
town hall meetings, picnics, receptions, Christmas 
parties, and birthday celebrations. 
Gloria likes to give, and she doesn't expect thanks. 
"If you call Gloria at the last minute and say you are 
desperate for two dozen cookies for a town meeting, 
she'll tell you not to worry and she will bake four 
dozen," swears a friend. 
She'll even share her 
recipes. 
"She'll do the jobs no one else wants to do," says 
another admirer. "She'll roll up her sleeves and 
pitch-in in the kitchen, or blow up all the balloons, 
make endless phone calls, find a OaI1Q--olllQ 
done with 
" 
"I adore Belvedere," Gloria 
"It's small, tranquil, and beautiful. I don't want it to 
change." This is a busy, unselfish woman 
ways to what needs to be done. She describes herself 
as "a Depression kid, growing up when everyone 
helped everyone else." 
In the aftermath of the winter storms in 1982, 
Gloria was the County-appointed 
liaison for Belvedere's senior 
citizens who lost property in the 
flood. She knew many because 
of her long-time residency in the 
community and the friendships 
she had made with them over the 
years. "There were a lot of them, 
she remembers, "whodidn 'tknow 
how to get their heat started again, 
or who were stranded. People 
need to help each 
" 
The honoree has lived on Cove 
Road with husband Irving for 
nearly 30 years. They have grown two children, 
David, who lives in San Francisco, and Joy, who 
lives in Madrid. 
They have two teenage 
grandchildren. 
Saying it's "been fun," Gloria has volunteered 
over the years in the hospitals, the schools, junior 
theater, 
is currently president of the Vine Village 
Guild which 
funds for a center for retarded 
young adults. 
project was bringing the Red 
service to our shores. "That was 
a ticket cost

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission Leslie Ann Carpentiers Deputy City Clerk 450 San Rafael Avenue Belvedere, CA 94920 Dear Ms. Carpentiers: September 7, 1988 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. 88-350 " Your letter requesting advice concerning the recently enacted campaign finance reform initiatives (Government Code section 85100, et ~) was received on September 6, 1988 by the Fair Political Practices commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. Because of the delayed operative date of most of the provisions of these initiatives, and the numerous inquiries we are receiving relative to implementation, we are attempting to respond to questions regarding interpretation in an organized and efficient fashion. Due to the volume of requests and complexity of the issues involved, we will answer your question in a timely manner, but not within the usual twenty-one working days. (Government Code Section 83114(b).) If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to information needed. You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. GWB:plh:3 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) ~22~5660 C:ITY of BELVEDERE State of California Memorandum To : File Date October 28, 1988 From : Fair Political Practices Commission John McLean Subject: T-88-350 Spoke today with Leslie Ann Carpentiers, Deputy city Clerk in Belvedere. Informed her about Emergency Regulation 18901 and the permanent regulation which the Commission will hear in December. Specifically informed her that: 1. Newsletters and mass mailings could not include articles written by Councilmembers if the articles listed the councilmembers as authors or contained the name, signature or other reference to an elected officer. 2. Newsletter articles on council actions could not include a name, signature or other reference to an elected officer. 3. Newsletters may not contain photos of elected officers. 4. Newsletters may contain a roster listing of elected officers in which all names appear in the same type size and typeface. BELVEDERE NEWSLETTER City of Belvedere • Incorporated 1897 450 SAN RAFAEL AVENUE BELVEDERE, CALIFORNIA 94920 Goals, Priorities and ments Let's go in this direction Shortly after the Council election April, the new City Council met to discuss goals and priorities for the upcoming fiscal year and to deter- mine Council assignments to various governmental committees and to various areas of City operations. Following several hours of dis- cussion, the City decided that the following were of top prior- ity: continued emphasis on improve- ments to the City storm drain system; exploration of Belvedere's participa- tion in efforts on the Tiburon penllnslula; velopment of a new pelummlll- emergency improvements to traffic safety; and completion of review the zoning and design review ordinances. Other issues acquisition of open space to Old St.
